title: CLI skeleton
description: Create pyxlsx/cli.py with: a top-level click group named 'cli', an output_result() helper that writes JSON to stdout by default or human-readable plain text when --plain is active, and a --plain flag on the root group. Define 'sheet', 'table', and 'cell' click subgroups as stubs. Include --version and --help.
acceptance_criteria: output_result() emits a single JSON object to stdout when --plain is not set
output_result() emits human-readable text when --plain is set
'sheet', 'table', and 'cell' subgroups are present as click groups even if empty

handoff_summary.completed: CLI skeleton created at .apm/skills/pyxlsx/pyxlsx/cli.py. --version prints 0.1.0, --help works, all three subgroups (sheet/table/cell) present, output_result() helper implemented.
handoff_summary.remaining: Downstream beads must implement actual commands under sheet/table/cell subgroups and wire ops/ modules. The --plain flag is on the root group and stored in ctx.obj["plain"] — downstream command implementations must use @click.pass_context to read it from the context object.
handoff_summary.risks: Downstream commands that implement the subgroups must use @click.pass_context and read ctx.obj["plain"] — or accept --plain locally. Commands that call output_result() need to pass the plain boolean themselves. The context-threading approach requires consistency across all command implementations.
handoff_summary.next_action: Tester agent should verify CLI skeleton via click.testing.CliRunner: --version, --help, subgroup presence, and output_result() JSON/plain behaviour.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: No conflicts expected. This file was new; no other bead claims cli.py.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: --plain is on the root group (not per-command) and stored in ctx.obj["plain"] via @click.pass_context. This matches the bead spec and allows a single flag position. Downstream commands must read ctx.obj["plain"] via pass_context.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: No tests written (developer role). Tester bead should cover: --version output, --help for cli/sheet/table/cell, output_result() JSON path, output_result() plain path.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: Subgroups are empty stubs — they will show no subcommands in --help until implementation beads add commands.

conflict_risks: No conflicts expected. This file was new; no other bead claims cli.py.

execution_history.summary: Created .apm/skills/pyxlsx/pyxlsx/cli.py with: top-level `cli` click group, `output_result()` helper (JSON default, plain text via plain_fn callback), `--plain` flag on the root group stored in click context, `--version` wired to pyxlsx.__version__ (0.1.0), and `sheet`, `table`, `cell` stub subgroups. All acceptance criteria verified via CliRunner.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `Python` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `python -m py_compile`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `pytest` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
